name: Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular from Tortosa
name_original: Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular desde Tortosa
region_intro: Road cycling around Tortosa offers diverse terrain, situated between the flat, wetland landscapes of the Ebro Delta Natural Park and the challenging, mountainous terrain of Els Ports Natural Park. The region is bisected by the Ebro River, providing scenic banks for cycling, and features dedicated greenways. This varied geography includes significant climbs, such as Mont Caro, and extensive networks of lightly trafficked roads. Best road cycling routes around Tortosa The most popular road cycling route is Summit of Mount Caro from Tortosa – Els Ports Natural Park, a 31.3 miles (50.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends to Mont Caro, the highest point in Els Ports Natural Park, offering significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular from Tortosa, a difficult 56.0 miles (90.2 km) path. This circular route explores the Sierras de Cardó-el Boix, presenting a challenging ride through varied landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Som Pass – Carretera dels Ports loop from Tortosa, a 73.7 miles (118.6 km) trail leading through the mountainous terrain of Els Ports, often completed in about 6 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Tortosa is defined by the flat expanses of the Ebro Delta, the challenging climbs of Els Ports Natural Park, and scenic routes along the Ebro River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Tortosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 2,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tortosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Tortosa?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes around Tortosa, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy, flat rides to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Tortosa?
A: Tortosa offers a highly diverse landscape for road cycling. To the east, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1533133/road-cycling-routes-around-tortosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Delta Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; provides flat, scenic routes through wetlands. To the west, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1533133/road-cycling-routes-around-tortosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Ports Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; presents challenging mountainous terrain with significant climbs and dramatic ravines. The Ebro River itself offers picturesque banks for cycling.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Tortosa has options for all skill levels. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are 4 easy road cycling routes available. The flat expanses of the Ebro Delta are particularly well-suited for less experienced cyclists or those looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What options are there for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Experienced road cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes, with 17 difficult options available. The climb to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970934346/summit-of-mount-caro-from-tortosa-els-ports-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Caro&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in Els Ports Natural Park, is a notable challenge with significant elevation gain. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40373685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Som Pass – Carretera dels Ports loop from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt; also offer extensive distances and considerable climbing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Tortosa?
A: Yes, many routes around Tortosa are designed as loops. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962759041/sierras-de-cardo-el-boix-circular-from-tortosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option. You can also find loops that incorporate mountain passes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40373685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Som Pass – Carretera dels Ports loop from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Tortosa offer access to stunning natural features and landmarks. You can explore the wetlands of the Ebro Delta, dramatic ravines in Els Ports Natural Park, and enjoy views of the Ebro River. Notable peaks and passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagalera Summit (1,035 m)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/786414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3987644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Som Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes with significant climbs and elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. The Els Ports Natural Park is renowned for its challenging climbs. The ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1074665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Caro&lt;/a&gt; from Tortosa is a prime example, known for its substantial elevation gain and inclusion in professional cycling races like the Volta de Catalunya. Several routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40373116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Som Pass – Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m) loop from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt;, feature over 2,000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Tortosa?
A: While many road cycling routes are geared towards more experienced riders, the flat terrain of the Ebro Delta and sections of the Val de Zafán Greenway offer easier, family-friendly cycling experiences. These areas provide safe, scenic paths away from heavy traffic.

Q: How well-rated are the road cycling routes in Tortosa by other cyclists?
A: The road cycling routes in Tortosa are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 2,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the region, often praising the diverse terrain and well-maintained paths.

Q: What is the 'Via Verda' and is it suitable for road cycling?
A: The 'Via Verda' refers to the Val de Zafán Greenway, an old railway line converted into a car-free path. It's an excellent option for cycling, featuring tunnels and viaducts. While it's generally suitable for various bikes, road cyclists might prefer sections with smoother surfaces, as some parts can be gravel. It offers a tranquil experience through varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tortosa?
A: The Terres de l'Ebre region, including Tortosa, is certified for bicycle touring, making it suitable for cycling year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential cooler, wetter conditions in winter. The region's mild climate means winter cycling is also possible.

Q: Are there any routes that offer great viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Tortosa provide spectacular viewpoints. The climb to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/786414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m)&lt;/a&gt; offers priceless panoramic views of the Ebro valley and Delta. Additionally, routes along the Ebro River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33756704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great view of the Ebro River – Som Pass loop from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt;, provide scenic vistas of the waterway and surrounding landscapes.
tips: Here you can enjoy an incredible view of the Ebro and the valley. We appreciate the surrounding vineyards and passes. As this segment takes place on a secondary road, parallel to a national road, it is not very frequented by cars.

This segment takes place on a road with little traffic. The climb allows you to appreciate a breathtaking view of the Ebro, the vegetation and the surrounding massifs. The descent is then welcome to relax the legs!

The Cathedral of Santa Maria de Tortosa is the seat of the diocese of the city of the same name in the province of Tarragona. Furthermore, it is thanks to the presence of the cathedral that Tortosa has gained its letters of nobility and has become a city in its own right. 

The building has a good mix of styles. Built on an old Romanesque colony from 1347, the style is mainly Gothic, although the façade shows a rather Baroque side, reinforced during its restoration. 

Located on the banks of the Ebro, the cathedral was left unfinished as higher towers would have blocked the cannons of the fortress on the hill.
